compiled_state.title: Brainstorm UX understanding loop: inject high-level design principles for UI features
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
Follow-on epic stub (scrutiny:pending). Requires /dso:brainstorm before implementation — scrutiny should run after the primary epic fdeb-4c03 (static UX probe loop) ships, and after reference library epic 0b7c-d811 (ref-query.sh + principles/ corpus) ships.

## Context
Once fdeb-4c03 establishes the static UX probe loop in brainstorm Phase 1 (Surface dimension, UI intent detection, three static probes, loop-back state machine, anti-redundancy directive), this epic replaces the static probe content with dynamic principle retrieval. Instead of a hardcoded probe set, the UX checkpoint queries ref-query.sh at Summary tier for principle-level entries relevant to the detected UI domain, and the agent synthesizes informed questions from the injected material (hybrid C path). The static probe set from fdeb-4c03 remains as the B fallback when ref-query returns fewer than N entries.

## Success Criteria (to be refined by /dso:brainstorm)
- UX checkpoint in brainstorm Phase 1 queries ref-query.sh at Summary tier, filtered to principle-level entries relevant to the detected UI domain (auth/forms/navigation/visual)
- When ref-query returns dense results, agent synthesizes informed UX questions from the injected principles (C path) and asks them as follow-ups in the Phase 1 dialogue
- When ref-query returns sparse results (below threshold), the static probe set from fdeb-4c03 fires instead (B fallback) — no duplicate questions
- Injected guidance appears in the brainstorm dialogue context before approach proposals
- Non-UI features bypass ref-query entirely; no retrieval cost on the non-UI path

## Dependencies
- Epic fdeb-4c03 (Brainstorm UX probe loop: static baseline) must complete first — provides the Phase 1 flow scaffolding, UI detection, loop-back machinery, anti-redundancy directive, and B fallback probe set.
- Epic 0b7c-d811 (UI/UX reference library) must complete first — provides ref-query.sh, the principles/ corpus, and the retrieval contract.
